articles.title: Four different alien species retrieved from crashed UFOs, claims former CIA researcher | Hindustan Times
articles.summary: Dr Hal Puthoff, an 89‑year‑old physicist who worked on CIA‑funded projects, said the United States secretly recovered four alien species from crashed UFOs over several decades. He listed the species as Grays, Nordics, Reptilians and Insectoids, noting that he had not seen them himself but relied on information from people with direct knowledge. Puthoff’s remarks echo former intelligence official David Grusch’s 2023 testimony that the U.S. had retrieved a non‑human craft and biologics, a claim that has sparked congressional debate. The Pentagon and the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office have repeatedly denied possessing alien technology or remains, and a 2024 review found no verified evidence of such recoveries. No physical evidence has been released, and online reactions range from enthusiasm for disclosure to skepticism about the plausibility of repeated alien crashes.

en.title: Four different alien species retrieved from crashed UFOs, claims former CIA researcher | Hindustan Times
en.summary: Dr Hal Puthoff, an 89‑year‑old physicist who worked on CIA‑funded projects, said the United States secretly recovered four alien species from crashed UFOs over several decades. He listed the species as Grays, Nordics, Reptilians and Insectoids, noting that he had not seen them himself but relied on information from people with direct knowledge. Puthoff’s remarks echo former intelligence official David Grusch’s 2023 testimony that the U.S. had retrieved a non‑human craft and biologics, a claim that has sparked congressional debate.

de.title: Vier verschiedene außerirdische Spezies aus abgestürzten UFOs geborgen, behauptet ein ehemaliger CIA-Forscher | Hindustan Times
de.summary: Dr. Hal Puthoff, ein 89-jähriger Physiker, der an von der CIA finanzierten Projekten arbeitete, sagte, die Vereinigten Staaten hätten über mehrere Jahrzehnte hinweg heimlich vier außerirdische Arten aus abgestürzten UFOs geborgen. Er listete die Arten als Graue, Nordische, Reptilien und Insektoide auf und bemerkte, dass er sie selbst nicht gesehen hatte, sondern sich auf Informationen von Menschen mit direktem Wissen verließ. Puthoffs Äußerungen spiegeln die Aussage des ehemaligen Geheimdienstmitarbeiters David Grusch aus dem Jahr 2023 wider, dass die USA ein nichtmenschliches Raumschiff und Biologika beschlagnahmt hätten, eine Behauptung, die eine Debatte im Kongress ausgelöst hat.

articles.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
articles.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released. The FBI is investigating a series of missing and dead scientists linked to secret nuclear and UFO programs, including the disappearance of retired General William Neil McCasland. Other scientists, such as David Wilcock, have also died under suspicious circumstances, adding to the pattern of unexplained losses.

en.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
en.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released.

de.title: Der Geheimagent, der UFO-Geheimnisse enthüllen wollte, starb vor fünf Jahren. Ein Whistleblower hat nun Verdacht geäußert
de.summary: Kevin Childress, ein 30-jähriger Kriminalermittler des Energieministeriums, starb 2021 unerwartet in seinem Haus in Georgia, wobei die Behörden Komplikationen durch COVID-19 anführten. UFO-Whistleblower Luis Elizondo behauptet, Childress sei vor seinem Tod gesund gewesen und habe gewarnt, dass das DOE eine wichtige Rolle in UAP-Programmen gespielt habe. Elizondo sagt, das Energieministerium versuche ihn zum Schweigen zu bringen und stellt fest, dass weder eine öffentliche Autopsie noch eine detaillierte Todesursache veröffentlicht wurden.

articles.title: Former Pentagon-Linked Physicist Alleges the United States Has Retrieved Biological Remains From Four Types of Alien Life Forms
articles.summary: Australian journalist Ross Coulthart claimed on X that President Donald Trump has been briefed on the legacy UAP crash‑retrieval program, sparking debate over when and how the briefing occurred. The claim follows rumors that Trump was briefed during his first term, including a December statement by whistleblower David Grusch that he had been informed about the full crash‑retrieval program. Dr. Eric Davis, former UAPTF science advisor, said he briefed Trump in late 2020 on UAPs but excluded the legacy program, and later accused Grusch of misspeaking, arguing the program was hidden through financial fraud and concealment. Reddit and X users questioned the evidence behind Coulthart’s claim, with some calling him gullible and suggesting a possible psyop, while others demanded concrete details and sources. The situation remains unresolved, with uncertainty about when Trump was briefed and what information he received, and the debate continues amid scrutiny of the Pentagon’s legacy program.

articles.title: US government releases UFO sighting reports - 'Orbs swarming in all directions'
articles.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.

en.title: US government releases UFO sighting reports - 'Orbs swarming in all directions'
en.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement.

de.title: US-Regierung veröffentlicht UFO-Sichtungsberichte – „Kugeln schwärmen in alle Richtungen“
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert bleibt und dass weitere Dateien fortlaufend veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
What does the second batch of UFO files tell us?

qualityBreakdown.label: Starker Titel
qualityBreakdown.text: Der Titel enthält einen klaren UAP/UFO-Bezug.

articles.qualityExplanation: Die Punkte zeigen UAP-Bezug, starke Begriffe, offizielle Stellen, Quellenvertrauen, mehrere Quellen und bei schwachen Einzelquellen einen vorsichtigen Abzug.

en.title: Aliens? Nephilim? Boebert Weighs in on UAPs
en.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement.

de.title: Außerirdische? Nephilim? Boebert äußert sich zu UAPs
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert bleibt und dass weitere Dateien fortlaufend veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

summaries.four-different-alien-species-retrieved-f: Dr Hal Puthoff, an 89‑year‑old physicist who worked on CIA‑funded projects, said the United States secretly recovered four alien species from crashed UFOs over several decades. He listed the species as Grays, Nordics, Reptilians and Insectoids, noting that he had not seen them himself but relied on information from people with direct knowledge. Puthoff’s remarks echo former intelligence official David Grusch’s 2023 testimony that the U.S. had retrieved a non‑human craft and biologics, a claim that has sparked congressional debate. The Pentagon and the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office have repeatedly denied possessing alien technology or remains, and a 2024 review found no verified evidence of such recoveries. No physical evidence has been released, and online reactions range from enthusiasm for disclosure to skepticism about the plausibility of repeated alien crashes.
summaries.the-2026-alien-soft-launch-missing-scien: Página não encontrada – a página solicitada não existe no site Folha do ES. O site exibe um cabeçalho com o título “Página não encontrada” e oferece links para a página inicial, política de privacidade, colunas e redes sociais. O rodapé indica que os direitos autorais pertencem ao Folha do ES em 2026. No corpo da página não há mais informações além de navegação e avisos de copyright.
summaries.secret-agent-who-was-about-to-blow-the-l: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released. The FBI is investigating a series of missing and dead scientists linked to secret nuclear and UFO programs, including the disappearance of retired General William Neil McCasland. Other scientists, such as David Wilcock, have also died under suspicious circumstances, adding to the pattern of unexplained losses.
summaries.program-former-pentagon-linked-physicist: Australian journalist Ross Coulthart claimed on X that President Donald Trump has been briefed on the legacy UAP crash‑retrieval program, sparking debate over when and how the briefing occurred. The claim follows rumors that Trump was briefed during his first term, including a December statement by whistleblower David Grusch that he had been informed about the full crash‑retrieval program. Dr. Eric Davis, former UAPTF science advisor, said he briefed Trump in late 2020 on UAPs but excluded the legacy program, and later accused Grusch of misspeaking, arguing the program was hidden through financial fraud and concealment. Reddit and X users questioned the evidence behind Coulthart’s claim, with some calling him gullible and suggesting a possible psyop, while others demanded concrete details and sources. The situation remains unresolved, with uncertainty about when Trump was briefed and what information he received, and the debate continues amid scrutiny of the Pentagon’s legacy program.
summaries.sighting-us-government-releases-ufo-sigh: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.

en.title: Four different alien species retrieved from crashed UFOs, claims former CIA researcher | Hindustan Times
en.summary: Dr Hal Puthoff, an 89‑year‑old physicist who worked on CIA‑funded projects, said the United States secretly recovered four alien species from crashed UFOs over several decades. He listed the species as Grays, Nordics, Reptilians and Insectoids, noting that he had not seen them himself but relied on information from people with direct knowledge. Puthoff’s remarks echo former intelligence official David Grusch’s 2023 testimony that the U.S. had retrieved a non‑human craft and biologics, a claim that has sparked congressional debate.

de.title: Vier verschiedene außerirdische Spezies aus abgestürzten UFOs geborgen, behauptet ein ehemaliger CIA-Forscher | Hindustan Times
de.summary: Dr. Hal Puthoff, ein 89-jähriger Physiker, der an von der CIA finanzierten Projekten arbeitete, sagte, die Vereinigten Staaten hätten über mehrere Jahrzehnte hinweg heimlich vier außerirdische Arten aus abgestürzten UFOs geborgen. Er listete die Arten als Graue, Nordische, Reptilien und Insektoide auf und bemerkte, dass er sie selbst nicht gesehen hatte, sondern sich auf Informationen von Menschen mit direktem Wissen verließ. Puthoffs Äußerungen spiegeln die Aussage des ehemaligen Geheimdienstmitarbeiters David Grusch aus dem Jahr 2023 wider, dass die USA ein nichtmenschliches Raumschiff und Biologika beschlagnahmt hätten, eine Behauptung, die eine Debatte im Kongress ausgelöst hat.

de.title: Der Alien Soft Launch 2026: Vermisste Wissenschaftler, Aliens.gov und Pentagon-Geheimnisse (y01QlMdFZnb)
de.summary: Die Seite wurde nicht gefunden – eine angeforderte Seite existiert auf dieser Seite nicht. Die Website ist mit dem Titel „Seite nicht gefunden“ ausgestattet und bietet Links zur ersten Seite, zur Datenschutzpolitik, zu Spalten und zu sozialen Inhalten. Wir weisen darauf hin, dass die Autoren seit 2026 für Folha verantwortlich sind.

en.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
en.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released.

de.title: Der Geheimagent, der UFO-Geheimnisse enthüllen wollte, starb vor fünf Jahren. Ein Whistleblower hat nun Verdacht geäußert
de.summary: Kevin Childress, ein 30-jähriger Kriminalermittler des Energieministeriums, starb 2021 unerwartet in seinem Haus in Georgia, wobei die Behörden Komplikationen durch COVID-19 anführten. UFO-Whistleblower Luis Elizondo behauptet, Childress sei vor seinem Tod gesund gewesen und habe gewarnt, dass das DOE eine wichtige Rolle in UAP-Programmen gespielt habe. Elizondo sagt, das Energieministerium versuche ihn zum Schweigen zu bringen und stellt fest, dass weder eine öffentliche Autopsie noch eine detaillierte Todesursache veröffentlicht wurden.

de.title: Ehemaliger mit dem Pentagon verbundener Physiker behauptet, die Vereinigten Staaten hätten biologische Überreste von vier Arten außerirdischer Lebensformen geborgen
de.summary: Der australische Journalist Ross Coulthart behauptete auf X, dass Präsident Donald Trump über das alte UAP-Absturzrettungsprogramm informiert worden sei, was eine Debatte darüber auslöste, wann und wie die Unterrichtung stattfand. Die Behauptung folgt Gerüchten, dass Trump während seiner ersten Amtszeit informiert wurde, einschließlich einer Erklärung des Whistleblowers David Grusch im Dezember, dass er über das vollständige Crash-Recovery-Programm informiert worden sei. Dr.

The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis.
